entry.title: Securing Your SAP Sales & Service Cloud V2+ESM Extensions with Application Vulnerability Report-Must
entry.content:
As organizations extend SAP Sales & Service Cloud V2 + ESM with custom solutions built on SAP BTP (Business Technology Platform), security becomes paramount. Whether you’re developing CAP-based services, prehooks/posthooks, custom APIs, Autoflow automations, or mashup services using JavaScript, Python, or Java—every extension must undergo rigorous security validation before reaching production environment (Extensions).

This blog explores how the Application Vulnerability Report (AVR) for CloudFoundry applications ensures your SAP Sales & Service Cloud V2 + ESM extensions are production-ready and free from critical security vulnerabilities.

What is the Application Vulnerability Report (AVR)?

The AVR is SAP’s built-in security scanning tool for CloudFoundry applications deployed on BTP. It automatically analyzes your applications for:

  • Known CVEs (Common Vulnerabilities and Exposures) in dependencies
  • Outdated libraries with security patches available
  • Configuration weaknesses in application manifests
  • Container image vulnerabilities (for Docker-based deployments)

Key Features:

  • Automated scanning upon deployment on weekly scan
  • Detailed findings with severity ratings (Critical, High, Medium, Low)
  • Remediation guidance with version upgrade recommendations
  • Compliance alignment with industry security standards

Reference: Introducing Application Vulnerability Report for CloudFoundry Applications

Why Security Scanning Matters for SAP Sales & Service Cloud V2 + ESM Side by Side Extensions

This checklist is mandatory—without any compromise—to ensure that no security vulnerabilities exist in your extensions before they are deployed to the Production environment.

SAP Sales & Service Cloud V2 +ESM serves as the backbone for customer-facing operations. 

Any extensions developed on BTP—whether for data enrichment, workflow automation, or third‑party integrations—introduce potential attack vectors if not properly secured.

Additionally, many side‑by‑side extensions that are developed by your developers, who may not always account for all required security controls to ensure the application or APIs are tightly governed. In some cases, extensions may also have been built using AI-assisted development, which can inadvertently introduce vulnerabilities in libraries or application logic, including risks such as XSS, CSRF, SSRF, and others.

Common Extension Scenarios Requiring AVR:

  • CAP (Cloud Application Programming Model) Services: Custom business logic exposing OData/REST APIs
  • Extensibility = External Hook (Prehooks & Posthooks) : Event-driven extensions modifying standard processes
  • Custom Microservices: Standalone services for specialized business functions
  • Autoflow Integrations: Low-code automations connecting external systems
  • Mashup Services: Composite applications combining multiple data sources

The Risk: Vulnerabilities in dependencies, libraries outdated, insecure coding practices, or misconfigurations can expose sensitive customer data or disrupt critical business processes.

Conclusion

The Application Vulnerability Report is non-negotiable for securing SAP Sales & Service Cloud V2 + ESM extensions. By integrating AVR into your development lifecycle, you:

  • Protect customer data from exploits
  • Maintain compliance with security standards
  • Ensuring No security vulnerabilities violated in your extensions designed for SAP Sales & Service Cloud V2 + ESM 
  • Build trust with stakeholders through proactive risk management

Call to Action for Developers and BTP Admins (Managing your BTP Landscape):

Before you move to production deployment, run AVR and clear all findings.
Your security depends on it.

entry.title: Re: Validation of Standard SAP TRM Interest Calculation v...
entry.content:
Hi

Interest calculation is complex and a lot of details are available based on selected interest calculation, Please check below link. 

SAP interest

In the standard Interest Rate Instrument (Product Category 550), the interest base is determined by the position's nominal value. SAP does not "look back" at the previous day’s accrual subledger to increase the calculation base for the current day.

Standard SAP interest calculation methods (like 360/360 or act/360) are designed for simple interest. True daily compounding requires the interest to be "capitalized" (added to the principal) to influence the next day's calculation.

newer S/4HANA release, look into Compound Interest Rates (often used for RFRs like SOFR or ESTR). SAP introduced new calculation logic for these where the system does support compounding, but it is specific to the "Variable Interest" setup and often calculates the compound factor at the end of the period rather than changing the daily principal.

You can manually add "Principal Increase" flows daily, but this is an administrative nightmare and highly discouraged.

entry.title: Re: Installation of Crystal Reports 2025 damages installa...
entry.content:
Hi Patrick,

That is normal behaviour for CRD and CR for VS and the reason why is....

Microsoft's MSIExec doesn't do a very good job when uninstalling so SAP created it's own installer using individual MSI files. When upgrading CRD what the installer is doing is uninstalling every file and then installing from scratch every file.

CR for VS still uses MSIExec to install, so uninstalling CRD can mess up CR for VS install.

What you discovered is due to the order of install, uninstalling CRD changes the install order of some common files, only way to fix it is to repair CR for VS install.

Of course this only happens when CRD and CR for VS are on the same PC.

Have a great day

Don

entry.title: Re: abapgit-agent: AI Coding Assistants for ABAP
entry.content:
🐛 New: dump and debug commands in abapgit-agent

Two new commands to help investigate ABAP runtime issues directly from the terminal (or via AI):

abapgit-agent dump — inspect ST22 short dumps without opening SAP GUI → docs

abapgit-agent debug — set breakpoints, attach, step through code, and inspect variables → docs

Both commands can be called automatically by AI agents — check out the troubleshooting section in abap/CLAUDE.md to see how Claude picks the right tool depending on whether there's a dump or a logic bug to trace.

See it in action: Claude used debug to trace how abapGit decides which files to pull and identified several bugs in the process.

entry.title: Re: How to create Business Users in SAP S/4HANA
entry.content:
Hi @chelliah_soundar ,

Could you please clarify if the below understanding is correct?

Scenario 1 – Vendor → Business Partner (CVI):

CVI: Creates a Vendor Business Partner
Vendor already exists in LFA1 and LFB1 table. So that remains the same.
Vendor number is linked to vendor BP in CVI_VEND_LINK table.
Scenario 2 – Employee Business Partner → Vendor (for newly hired employees)

An Employee Business Partner & Employment Business Partner is created first
FI Vendor role is added. 
The system also automatically creates a vendor record in LFA1, LFB1 tables. Then also creates an entry in CVI_VEND_LINK table.

Thanks & Regards,
Karthi

entry.title: Re: Technical validation request: multiple copies of the...
entry.content:
1. Is the caller prepared to handle errors/warnings returned by the SAP system?

    1. Yes, the customer already works this way today. However, they are currently using ECC on-premise and will be moving to RISE with SAP.

       

  2. Why do the calls need to be bound through the entire SD end-to-end to a single instance if they are copies? 
    1. Because the customer uses different systems for testing, as if they were separate test instances, each feedback cycle needs to be handled in the corresponding instance.
       
       
       
  3. IFlows cannot be deployed more than once because the IDs need to be unique, which means you would require 10 copies of each IFlow that is designed - that sounds like a maintenance nightmare IMO.
    1. I agree. My suggestion was to keep a 1:1 approach. However, the customer already manages it this way today, and currently they do it through a redirection/repointing process, which already creates a significant amount of manual effort.

entry.title: Re: MTO and STO (Scope item 1P9)
entry.content:
Good day @Moon3 

Thank you for your question.

You can use MTO and you can use Intercompany Stock Transfer in Public Cloud, but you cannot combine them in the way you described. The point to note is, Scope item 1P9 is a stock transfer process. It stars from a purchase order in the receiving company and drives deliveries and goods movement. It is not a sales order driven MTO process, so an STO does not trigger a Sales Order in standard 1P9.

See Blog: Intercompany Stock Transfer (1P9) in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition for more insights.

Here are answers to your main question.

Question: Is there any way that I also use Intercompany stock transfer process ( scope item 1P9) with MTO.

Answer: Not as “STO first, then SO, then MRP”. In 1P9, the receiving company creates the intercompany PO and the supplying company performs delivery and billing steps. There is no standard step where the system automatically creates a customer sales order from that STO in classis 1P9.

In Public Cloud, you can achieve MTO + intercompany supply by using a sales order driven intercompany scenario where the Sales Order serves as the trigger and the system creates follow on purchasing or supplying documents accordingly. In other words, the correct direction flow for MTO is as follows:

SO --> triggers supply from affiliate plant --> planning/procurement/production in supplying entity --> delivery --> billing.

If your requirement is specifically for SO to trigger intercompany procurement and production, then you should evaluate Advance Intercompany Sales scope content.

See the following reference for more insights

Exploring the Advanced Intercompany Stock Transfer (5HP) Scenario

See Blog: Advanced Intercompany Sales & Stock Transfer in SAP S/4HANA Cloud for more details.

Question: But I do not know how STO can trigger SO?

Answer: It does not in classic 1P9. The reason is, 1P9 is designed as an intercompany stock transfer flow and not an intercompany customer order flow.

If you really need an SO to be created in supplying company, that is typically covered by advanced intercompany processes, not classic 1P9.

My recommendation is to use a sales order driven intercompany scenario. If it is MTO with production, evaluated the advanced intercompany sales MTO scope content.

Alternatively, you can keep 1P9 purely for stock transfer with no SO. The design will remain:

  • HQ creates STO
  • Supplying entity delivers
  • Receiving entity receives

 

Let me know if you have further questions.

Best regards

Chris

entry.title: Re: No receivers were found for segment error When Using...
entry.content:
Good day @R_Pooja 

Thank you for your question.

From your screenshot, the system message typically means it did not find any receiver records that match your Receiver Basis and Receiver Tracing Factor selection for your defined period, so it cannot weight the variable portions.

The Public Cloud offers you the possibility for:

  • Variable Portion plus Receiver Basis equalling Actual Quantities, but it only works if the quantity field group you selected actually has receiver quantity values in the relevant period and the receiver selection matches those postings.
  • If the quantity is posted somewhere else (wrong field group, wrong ledger, wrong period, etc.), the Manage Allocations will return no receivers found. See KBA 3609752 for more insights.

See the following references for more details

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/1cbcff7ccd35405ab445b223c1ab1588/cb2b3307a05c4d669d04d2bf13c26b38.html?locale=en-US https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/f0d042517a434458b171ff5867b18445/51f37e43ceab4444b752111f7868a482.html?locale=en-US https://userapps.support.sap.com/sap/support/knowledge/en/3407708

So, what you are seeing is common for Variable Portion with Actual Quantities if the selected field group and selection criteria do not point to quantity postings that are attributed to the receiver object.

Please validate in the segment that the Receiver Basis Type is Actual Quantities and the correct field group is selected, then verify in Manage Journal Entries that the receiver basis GL account line items have both as receiver cost center and a posted quantity in the same period and ledger. If the quantity is posted without the receiver assignment, the allocation will not recognise receivers.

Let me know if you have further questions.

Best regards

Chris

entry.title: Re: Purpose of Field Group Status “Actual Quantities” in...
entry.content:
Good day @R_Pooja 

Thank you for your question.

In Public Cloud, Field Groups in Manage Allocations define which fields and measures the allocation engine reads when it calculates sender values and receiver tracing factors. The “Actual Quantities“ Field Group status indicated that the segment is intended to use posted actual quantity measures as the basis for receiving weighting. Basically saying, which fields and measures the allocation engine is allowed to read for building:

  • Sender values (what you allocate), and
  • Receiver tracing factors (how you split it).

See the following reference for more details

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/1cbcff7ccd35405ab445b223c1ab1588/4adf8178f6a940579e766311ca91fdf8.html?locale=en-US

 

Regarding how it influences allocations when you use Variable Portion à Actual Quantity:

When you set a segment like this:

  • Receiver Rule = Variable Portions
  • Receiver Basis Type = Actual Quantities

The system uses actual quantities to calculate the receiver split:

  1. It reads receiver relevant postings that match your Receiver Basis selection (period, ledger, company code, basis account, etc).
  2. It sums the actual quantities per receiver (for example per cost center) using the chosen Field Group.
  3. It allocates sender amounts proportionally as follow:
  • Receiver share = Receiver quantity divided by Total quantity
  • Allocated amount = Sender amount multiplied by Receiver share.

In summary, when you use Variable Portions with Actual Quantities Receiver Basis Type, the system sums actual quantities per receiver and allocates sender values proportionally to each receiver’s quantity share. If the selected field group or selection criteria do not point to posting where both the receiver object and quantities exist, the allocation cannot determine receiver shares.

See the following reference for more insights

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/f0d042517a434458b171ff5867b18445/4c8999e494f14169b52ccb1f588c4de2.html?locale=en-US

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/1cbcff7ccd35405ab445b223c1ab1588/cb2b3307a05c4d669d04d2bf13c26b38.html?locale=en-US

 

Let me know if you have further questions.

Best regards

Chris

entry.title: Claude Code via MCP: Poor man's Joule, or a practical tool?
entry.content:
In my previous blog posts (1) and (2) I described vibe coding in ABAP with Claude Code and OpenAI Codex by means of Node.js-based MCP Servers. I today's blog I'll use an MCP Server built by Alice Vinogradova: https://github.com/oisee/vibing-steampunk?trk=article-ssr-frontend-pulse_little-text-block.

Alice's solution is different in that:

  1. It installs as a single binary,
  2. it is more clever: It tries to unburden the AI by taking care of certain routine operations (such as locking / unlocking Objects for changes), so that AI can concentrate on the stuff that matters.

Here is Alice's oft-quoted article in which she explains her approach better than I ever could.

Spoiler: I cannot confirm with 100% certainty that Alice's solution improves AI capabilities, but I saw strong empirical evidence to that effect.

As usual, I asked an Agent in Antigravity to do the installation:

Then I used Claude Code CLI to do various changes to an SALV Report I had previously created with Codex (a simple Report with some travel data from a DB Table I created time ago for the RAP100 course). In particular, I asked Claude to:

  • Add Customer Name, Agency Name and Status Description to the Report. For this, Claude had to figure out additional data sources.
  • Add a select "box" column to the ALV.
  • Enable selection on an individual cell level in the ALV.
  • Display Trip Prices > 10.000 of any Currency in red.
  • Add ALV Layout to the Selection Screen. If no Layout selected, then display the results totalled and subtotalled in a certain way.
  • Transform the SELECT Statement in the Report into a CDS View, then use that CDS View.

Here are some conversation examples:

You will notice that Claude went above and beyond by also adding F4 Help to the Layout Field in the Selection Screen.

A keen eye might also notice that I switched to the lesser Sonnet (4.6) model so as not to keep running into rate limits all the time. I didn't observe any degradation of performance, which might at least in part be explained by Alice's very excellent work on reducing the model's mental load by what she calls "composite tools" (again: read the article).

You see that I kept my prompting deliberately vague. "Use the same Package and Transport Request for the CDS View." Like, the same one as the Program uses, or what? None of this threw Claude off.

 

One of the use cases Alice mentions for her MCP Server is data analysis, and I wanted to test it. I asked Claude questions like "How many trips more expensive than 10000 EUR are there?", without specifying any tool or Program or data source to use. He derived these things from the context of our conversation and responded correctly.

Then I asked "How many of these are holidays, how many business trips?" 

See what I did there? Used the word "holidays" when the table entries are described as "Vacation", "Sightseeing in Frankfurt", or "Visiting Pierre".

Claude categorised the trips correctly, even though in the end he used a rather simplistic (though perfectly reasonable) approach: Everything starting with "Business" is a business trip, everything else is holidays.

To make Claude's life more difficult, I changed descriptions of 3 trips from "Business Trip" to: "Client visit", "IT conference" and "Visiting company Branch in Tulsa". This tripped him up - he simply applied the logic that had served him so well previously, and mis-categorised these 3 Trips as leisure. I pointed out the mistake and asked him to look closely at the descriptions. Then he got it right.

So much for some very simple examples of data analysis.

Next I wanted to come back to the RAP / Fiori app topic. I asked Claude to scaffold a RAP Demo using the Northwind Traders Database model originally from Microsoft - the simplified version. He searched the web for info and then created the following artefacts. All by his lonesome, in about 30 minutes.

I have yet to look at them, but I do know one thing for sure: The RAP Application does not work. I suspect the reason might be that Claude forgot to include in the underlying Database Tables the Fields 

  • LOCAL_LAST_CHANGED_BY,
  • LOCAL_LAST_CHANGED_AT,
  • LAST_CHANGED_AT, 

which are vital for the locking mechanism in RAP Managed scenarios.

Or it could be something else. In any case, this is a typical bit of knowledge that would go into a Claude Skill. I hear Skills are much improved in their Version 2 which just came out couple of days ago - apparently, they are now testable, and you can pretty much guarantee that they will be invoked.

It seems Skills are misunderstood in the same way as MCPs were a year ago (if you hear someone say that MCP is like the USB-C port but for AI, you are now legally allowed to punch them in the face, did you know that?)  What is especially misunderstood about Skills is their Progressive Disclosure feature - people write 1000-line Skill files and then wonder why their model got dumber. We ABAPers wouldn't do that, because we know about modularisation, amiright? Except those few who still write God Classes with 13000 lines of code. But they are not here - they don't read technology blogs.

So what do you guys think, is Claude / Codex via MCP just a poor man's Joule, or might it aspire to fill the large Joule-shaped hole in our lives to at least some extent?

 

P.S.: No AI was involved in writing the texts of these Blog posts. If it had been, you wouldn't know it, because I would have found the Wikipedia page on AI-sounding writing, fed it to the AI and instructed it to avoid those patterns. But I didn't 😊 

 

Or didn't I.

I'll guess we'll never know.

entry.title: Re: OBYC is maintained after that Account determination f...
entry.content:
Dear @deepa_khatri,

This behavior is expected in both Public and Private Cloud. SAP determines the G/L account using an exact combination of values such as transaction key, account grouping code, valuation grouping code, valuation class and chart of accounts. For goods movements the account grouping code is used to further split postings, especially for transaction key GBB depending on the movement type and other indicators. Therefore, if the posting is derived with a blank account modifier but configuration is maintained only for VAX or VAY the system will not find a matching entry and an error is expected

More details below:

https://learning.sap.com/courses/configuring-supply-chain-business-scenarios-in-sap-s4hana-cloud-public-edition/setting-up-account-determination_c2813e02-88a0-422f-af4a-28bc70b3179b

https://learning.sap.com/courses/cross-functional-customizing-in-sap-s-4hana-materials-management/subdividing-a-transaction-with-the-account-grouping-code

VAX and VAY are not generic substitutes for a blank modifier. SAP uses them for specific sales-order-related scenarios. VAY when the sales order item carries costs and revenues and VAX when it does not. If your current posting is not one of those scenarios then using a blank modifier can be fully correct

More details below:

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_ON-PREMISE/5e23dc8fe9be4fd496f8ab556667ea05/1e20ba538c95b54ce10000000a174cb4.html

So, maintaining the account modifier as blank is not wrong by itself. It simply means the posting will use the more general account determination line, instead of being split to a separate G/L account through a dedicated modifier such as VAX or VAY         

Summary:

Keep the blank entry if that is the key your business transaction actually uses and maintain VAX/VAY only as additional entries for the specific sales-order scenarios where SAP derives those modifiers. Do not replace a required blank entry with VAX/VAY

Best Regards,

Dawid

entry.title: SAP Business One 10 Upgrade From 2308 to 2602
entry.content:
I am trying to upgrade SAP Business One 10 from 2308 to 2602. When I try to run the setup.exe, I was able to connect to SLD and it upgraded the client, DTW, SDK etc. and failed in SLD.  I had the backup of SLD and tried to run the setup.exe again, Now I am unable to connect SLD from both setup.exe and from web. I am struck and I am not sure, how to fix this issue.  I am getting the below error message from setup.exe and when I try to access from web, I am getting blank page. 

Cannot connect to SLD Server: Verify site user password or check proxy server connection settings

I am entering the right password and there is no proxy server in our environment. 

Please help.

entry.title: SAP Stammtisch | SAP Community Meetup (Cologne #5)
entry.content:
Hallo Zusammen,

nächste Woche starten die DSAG Technologietage und der TechXchange in Hamburg. Direkt danach am Freitag, den 20. März,  findet SAP Stammtisch #5 in Köln statt, falls du noch Energie nach der Woche hast, komm gern vorbei uns lass uns über die Themen der Tage diskutieren. Um 18 Uhr treffen wir uns im CAFE ESPECIAL, direkt beim Bahnhof Messe/Deutz. Wir freuen uns auf dich und falls du zum ersten Mal dabei bist, es wird ein entspannter Abend ohne Zwang und mit offenen Ohren.

Grüße
Orga-Team

###

Hello everyone,

next week, the DSAG Technology Days and TechXchange kick off in Hamburg. Immediately afterward, on Friday 20th March, SAP Community Meetup #5 will take place in Cologne. If you still have some energy after the week, feel free to join us and let's discuss the topics from the event. We'll meet at 6 p.m. at CAFE ESPECIAL, right by the Messe/Deutz train station. We look forward to seeing you, and if this is your first time, it will be a relaxed evening with no pressure and an open mind.

Best regards,

The Organizing Team

entry.title: 🇪🇸 Joule Studio: Create Joule Skills and Agents Without Coding (Madrid, Spain)
entry.content:
This is the all-new SAP CodeJam for Joule Studio, the new tool in SAP Build that lets you create extensions to Joule so users can use SAP's digital assistant in their custom workflows.

This CodeJam will take place the day before of the SIT Madrid 2026. For more information, check out https://sitmadrid.com. You can RSVP for the SIT Madrid here.

The CodeJam is intended to introduce you to the Joule Studio tool, so you can create your own:

  • Skills, which let you create predefined execution flows, triggered by a user's prompt inside the Joule chatbot
  • Agents, which lets you define complex scenarios that the agent can execute by iteratively reasoning and interacting with their environment to achieve a goal, using a selected LLM, and triggered by a user's prompt inside the Joule chatbot

The session will include a short, simple introduction to the SAP AI tools and some basic concepts needed for working with Joule Studio – such as AI Core, document grounding, embeddings, and what after all is an LLM.

Currently, the event is based on these tutorials: Joule Studio CodeJam

Prerequisites

  • Bring your own laptop with power adapter and have a supported Web browser of either Google Chrome or Microsoft Edge. Other browsers are not supported.
  • It can be helpful to bring a tablet or second laptop, if you have, to have the instructions on a separate screen.

Location and Date 

This is an in-person event only (not virtual) and is planned for Thursday, April 16, 2026 from 09:30 - 14:00 local time in Madrid, Spain. The language of the event and the content will be English.

Event Address: NEORIS Artificial Intelligence Hub, powered by EPAM, Calle de María de Portugal, Madrid, Spain 

  • The event is kindly being hosted by EPAM Neoris
  • The instructors are SAP Developer Advocates @ajmaradiaga and @Dan_Wroblewski.

Please note that spaces are limited! We can only accommodate 30 attendees. If you want to attend, register by clicking the Yes button in the Will you be attending? section above to the right. About 2 weeks before the event, you will be asked to confirm your attendance and will receive further instructions.

entry.title: New POD 2.0 Framework: Other Business Capabilities Enabled by POD 2.0
entry.content:
This blog post is part of a series on SAP Digital Manufacturing new POD 2.0 framework, the new generation of SAP Digital Manufacturing worker UIs.

New POD 2.0 Framework

  1. The New Generation of SAP Digital Manufacturing Worker UIs
  2. Template-Based Design Instead of POD Types
  3. Visual WYSIWYG Designer
  4. POD 2.0 Hands-on, Flexible Layout Capabilities, Part 1
  5. POD 2.0 Hands-on, Flexible Layout Capabilities, Part 2
  6. Custom UI Dialogs and Calling Production Processes
  7. Dynamic PODs, How to Dynamically Show, Hide, Enable, and Disable UI Elements

🗓️ Timeline

POD 2.0 is currently in BETA with selected customers, General Availability (GA) is planned for 2605 release. BETA content is subject to change!

📢 Other Business Capabilities Enabled by POD 2.0

Electronic Batch Records (EBR)

Critical for regulated industries such as life sciences and medical devices, enabling structured operator workflows and traceability.

The Electronic Batch Record (EBR) capability enables the creation, review, and approval of document-driven batch records directly within SAP Digital Manufacturing. It supports structured batch documentation with role-based authorization, versioning, and a comprehensive audit trail, enabling compliance-focused production scenarios. This addresses the challenge of managing batch documentation across multiple systems or through manual, paper-based processes.

This way, EBR improves compliance with regulatory requirements such as GMP while reducing manual effort and documentation errors. It also establishes a consistent digital record that increases transparency, traceability, and readiness for audits.

Design Time

Run Time

Harmonized Component Consumption

Bridging discrete and process execution logic under a unified interaction model.

Design Time for the Harmonized Consume Component Dialog

Design Time for Order POD

Run Time for Order POD

Design Time for Work Center POD

Run Time for Work Center POD

Advanced Barcode / Data Scanning

Multi-data barcode capture that feeds automation, labeling, and backend integration flows.

The data scan capability enables barcode-driven execution of production activities directly within the POD by configuring barcode parsing and mapping rules. Scanned barcodes can be interpreted using predefined structures and automatically trigger actions such as assembly, data collection, or SFC start, without additional manual steps. This addresses the inefficiency and error risk of manual data entry and navigation during execution.

From a business standpoint, Data Scan reduces operator interaction time, accelerates production steps, and improves data accuracy on the shop floor. It also provides a scalable foundation for automation by reusing configurations across multiple execution scenarios.

Configuration Time

Using new Configure Data Scanning app.

Design Time

Run Time

These capabilities are built on POD 2.0 and represent the direction of future execution functionality.

Stay tuned for more! Thanks,
Manoel Costa

entry.title: Advanced intercompany stock transfer with valuated stock-in-transit(VSiT) for difference in receipt
entry.content:
Valuated stock-in-transit (VSiT) means that the goods have left the plant of the seller physically, but the ownership (title or risk) of goods has not yet been transferred from the seller to the buyer i.e the seller still holds ownership of goods. Ownership is transferred at some point during the transit generally at the incoterm location or at a location mutually agreed between the seller and buyer.

Advanced intercompany stock transfer uses VSiT to transfer the ownership of goods between delivering company , intermediate company (in case of multistage ) and receiving company.

There was a limitation in confirmation of goods receipt with difference in quantity for EWM managed storage location till S/4HANA2025 release as stated in SAP note 1822497 as in below image. This limitation has been eliminated in 2025 FPS01 release. My present blog is to discuss the new feature in context of advanced intercompany stock transfer(This new feature also works with classic intercompany stock transfer and intracompany stock transfer).

A typical process flow for VSiT with advanced intercompany stock transfer with delivering company 1810 US and receiving company 1010 DE is depicted in below image.

An Intercompany purchase order 4500003515 with quantity 5pc of purchase order type NBIC (IC SiT STO) is created at receiving plant 1010 with EWM managed storage location EROD at purchasing organization 1010 DE receiving company 1010 DE with intercompany vendor BP1810 representing delivering company 1810 US.

An outbound delivery 80004929 of delivery type NCC3 is created at US delivering plant 1810 with storage location UAFS.

Goods issue is executed on the corresponding outbound delivery order (ODO) 80004931 at EWM warehouse U002 at delivering company US.

Goods issue on EWM ODO 80004931 triggered PGI (Post gods issue) on LE delivery 80004931 which subsequently triggered VSiT postings (with movement types 681, 685, 107) and created inbound delivery 180001751 as shown with app Monitor value chains.

 

The inbound delivery 180001751 is created automatically with delivery type ELST (IDel. Val. Rec. SiT) at receiving plant 1010 DE with storage location EROD .

The new feature of 2025 FPS01 allows receipt of different quantity with exception code like DIFW (Diff. as Charges for Warehouse) but not with DIFD (Diff. as Charges for Inbound Delivery) as DIFD updates delivery quantity. A warehouse task is created on the inbound delivery  180001751 and the error Quantity change is not allowed with valuated SIT in an STO process.Message No. /SCWM/DELIVERY758 appears when confirming 4 pc for delivery quantity 5 pc with exception code DIFD.

System accepts the confirmation of 4 pc with exception code DIFW.

Difference in quantity needs to be updated with difference analyzer /SCWM/DIFF_ANALYZER.

The pre-requisite configuration to achieve the functionality is to flag the new checkbox “Prevent quantity changes for stock transport order” for the inbound item type IDLV for document category PDI.

There isa new field at the item level of the inbound delivery indicating the STO type (E Advanced intercompany process with valuated stock in transit).

Purchase order history shows the relevant movement types for VSiT.

This blog is based on my personal tests , observations , insights in S/4HANA 2025 FPS01 release. Will appreciate your feedback / comments.

entry.title: SAP Cloud Integration (CI/CPI) Dynamic Custom Headers for Monitoring - Config Driven Approach
entry.content:
When working with SAP Cloud Integration (CI/CPI), one of the common questions from operations teams is:

“How can we quickly add a new custom header to Message Monitoring without transporting the changes of iFlow?”

Maybe you need to filter messages by Delivery Number, Shipping Point, Sales Order, or something else that wasn’t planned during development.
With the standard approach, you have to modify the iFlow, add a custom header  property, transport the change through the landscape, and redeploy. This takes time and coordination.

The approach described in this post solves that problem by externalizing the monitoring rules in a governed JSON configuration, while the extraction logic remains in a reusable Script Collection.
This lets you add new monitoring fields at runtime, in a controlled and maintainable way.

What this solution enables?

  • Add/remove monitoring fields without updating each iFlow
  • Fully governed: all rules are controlled via a single JSON config
  • Centralized script in a Script Collection (no duplication)
  • Supports both XML and JSON payloads
  • Multiple extraction styles supported (XPath, deep search, JSONPath-lite)
  • Values appear in Message Monitoring as Custom Header Properties, which can be searched and filtered

    SAP’s documentation confirms that MPL custom header properties can be written via scripting, which makes them available for searching in the monitor.

    (Reference: https://help.sap.com/docs/cloud-integration/sap-cloud-integration/use-custom-header-properties-to-search-for-message-processing-logs?locale=en-US)

    SAP also recommends using Script Collections for reuse, central maintenance, and to avoid duplicating scripts across integration flows.

    (Reference: https://help.sap.com/docs/cloud-integration/sap-cloud-integration/developing-script-and-script-collection?locale=en-US&q=Script+Collection)

    Architecture Overview:

 

The design has three simple moving parts:

1) Global JSON Configuration (Governed)

A common iflow is used to write a global variable with global scope. The value(externalized) is a JSON document which defines custom header properties needed for respective iFlows to be monitored in the message monitoring.

Example JSON configuration:

{
  "root": {
    "Iflow": [
      {
        "IflowID": "OutboundDelivery_-_Sample",
        "HeaderDetails": [
          {
            "HeaderName": "DeliveryNumber",
            "HeaderPath": "DeliveryNumber"
          },
          {
            "HeaderName": "ShippingPoint",
            "HeaderPath": "//ShippingPoint"
          }  
        ]
      },
       {
        "IflowID": "SalesOrder_-_Sample",
        "HeaderDetails": [
          {
            "HeaderName": "SalesOrderNumber",
            "HeaderPath": "SalesOrderNumber"
          }  
        ]
      }
    ]
  }
}

 

You can easily add more fields or more iFlows to this JSON without touching any of the iFlows if new custom header properties are needed.

2) iFlow Property: HeaderConfigJson

Each iFlow loads the JSON config into an exchange property called HeaderConfigJson. Here, OutboundDelivery - Sample is an iFlow in which I want to add CustomHeaderProperties to monitor them in message monitoring.

3) Reusable Script Collection

A Groovy script (dynamicCustomHeaders.groovy) stored in a Script Collection reads the config, extracts values from the payload, and writes them into MPL custom header properties.

This way, each iFlow remains clean: it only needs one property assignment + one Script step.

Step-by-Step Implementation

Step 1: Create a Script Collection

  1. Go to your Integration Package
  2. Add a Script Collection artifact
  3. Upload the reusable script dynamicCustomHeaders.groovy
  4. Deploy the Script Collection
import com.sap.gateway.ip.core.customdev.util.Message
import groovy.json.JsonSlurper

import org.apache.camel.Exchange
import org.apache.camel.builder.SimpleBuilder

import javax.xml.parsers.DocumentBuilderFactory
import javax.xml.xpath.XPathFactory
import javax.xml.xpath.XPathConstants
import javax.xml.namespace.NamespaceContext

import org.w3c.dom.NodeList

Message processData(Message message) {

    // --- iFlow runtime id (camelId) -> stored in MPL as custom header property
    Exchange exchange = message.exchange
    String runtimeId = SimpleBuilder.simple('${camelId}')
            .evaluate(exchange, String)
            ?.toString()
            ?.trim()

    def messageLog = messageLogFactory?.getMessageLog(message)
    if (messageLog != null && runtimeId) {
        messageLog.addCustomHeaderProperty('IFlowId', runtimeId)
    }

    // --- Read config JSON from exchange property
    String cfgText = message.getProperty("HeaderConfigJson") as String
    if (!cfgText || cfgText.trim().isEmpty()) {
        return message
    }

    def cfg
    try {
        cfg = new JsonSlurper().parse(new StringReader(cfgText))
    } catch (Exception e) {
        // Invalid config -> do nothing in production (or throw if you prefer)
        return message
    }

    def iflowList = cfg?.root?.Iflow
    if (!iflowList) return message
    if (!(iflowList instanceof List)) iflowList = [iflowList]

    def selectedIflow = iflowList.find { it?.IflowID?.toString()?.trim()?.equalsIgnoreCase(runtimeId) }
    if (!selectedIflow) selectedIflow = iflowList[0]

    def headerDetails = selectedIflow?.HeaderDetails
    if (!headerDetails) return message
    if (!(headerDetails instanceof List)) headerDetails = [headerDetails]

    // --- Detect payload type
    String payload = message.getBody(String) as String
    if (!payload || payload.trim().isEmpty()) return message
    String p = payload.trim()

    boolean isXml  = p.startsWith("<")
    boolean isJson = p.startsWith("{") || p.startsWith("[")

    if (!isXml && !isJson) return message

    // ---------------- JSON helpers (used only if JSON payload) ----------------
    def deepFindValuesByKey
    deepFindValuesByKey = { Object obj, String key, List acc ->
        if (obj instanceof Map) {
            obj.each { k, v ->
                if (k?.toString() == key) acc << v
                deepFindValuesByKey(v, key, acc)
            }
        } else if (obj instanceof List) {
            obj.each { item -> deepFindValuesByKey(item, key, acc) }
        }
        return acc
    }

    def evalJsonPath = { Object root, String path ->
        if (!path) return []

        // Deep search: //KeyName
        if (path.startsWith("//")) {
            String key = path.substring(2)
            def found = deepFindValuesByKey(root, key, [])
            def flat = []
            found.each { v ->
                if (v instanceof List) flat.addAll(v)
                else flat << v
            }
            return flat
        }

        // JSONPath-lite: $.a.b[0].c or $.a[*].b
        if (!path.startsWith("\$.")) return []

        def tokens = path.substring(2).split("\\.")
        def current = [root]

        tokens.each { t ->
            def next = []
            def mIndex = (t =~ /^([^\[]+)\[(\d+|\*)\]$/)

            if (mIndex.matches()) {
                String name = mIndex[0][1]
                String idx  = mIndex[0][2]

                current.each { c ->
                    def node = (c instanceof Map) ? c[name] : null
                    if (node instanceof List) {
                        if (idx == "*") {
                            next.addAll(node)
                        } else {
                            int i = Integer.parseInt(idx)
                            if (i >= 0 && i < node.size()) next << node[i]
                        }
                    }
                }
            } else {
                current.each { c ->
                    if (c instanceof Map && c.containsKey(t)) {
                        next << c[t]
                    } else if (c instanceof List) {
                        c.each { item ->
                            if (item instanceof Map && item.containsKey(t)) next << item[t]
                        }
                    }
                }
            }

            current = next
        }

        def out = []
        current.each { v ->
            if (v instanceof List) out.addAll(v)
            else out << v
        }
        return out
    }

    // ---------------- XML setup (used only if XML payload) ----------------
    def doc = null
    def xPath = null
    String rootNsUri = ""

    def toLocalNameXPath = { String plainXPath ->
        if (!plainXPath) return plainXPath

        // //TagName -> //*[local-name()='TagName']
        if (plainXPath.startsWith("//")) {
            String tail = plainXPath.substring(2)
            if (tail && !tail.contains("/") && !tail.contains(":") && !tail.contains("(") && !tail.contains("@")) {
                return "//*[local-name()='" + tail + "']"
            }
        }

        // TagName -> //*[local-name()='TagName']
        if (!plainXPath.startsWith("/") && !plainXPath.startsWith("//")) {
            return "//*[local-name()='" + plainXPath + "']"
        }

        // /A/B[1]/C -> /*[local-name()='A']/*[local-name()='B'][1]/*[local-name()='C']
        def parts = plainXPath.tokenize('/')
        def sb = new StringBuilder()

        parts.each { part ->
            if (!part) return

            if (part.startsWith("@") || part.contains("(") || part == "." || part == ".." || part == "*") {
                sb.append("/").append(part)
                return
            }

            def m = (part =~ /^([^\[]+)(\[\d+\])?$/)
            if (m.matches()) {
                def qname = m[0][1]
                def idx   = m[0][2] ?: ""
                def local = qname.contains(":") ? qname.substring(qname.indexOf(":") + 1) : qname
                sb.append("/*[local-name()='").append(local).append("']").append(idx)
            } else {
                sb = new StringBuilder(plainXPath)
            }
        }

        return sb.toString()
    }

    Object jsonObj = null
    if (isJson) {
        try {
            jsonObj = new JsonSlurper().parseText(p)
        } catch (Exception e) {
            return message
        }
    }

    if (isXml) {
        try {
            def dbf = DocumentBuilderFactory.newInstance()
            dbf.setNamespaceAware(true)
            doc = dbf.newDocumentBuilder().parse(new ByteArrayInputStream(p.getBytes("UTF-8")))

            rootNsUri = doc?.documentElement?.namespaceURI ?: ""
            xPath = XPathFactory.newInstance().newXPath()

            xPath.setNamespaceContext(new NamespaceContext() {
                String getNamespaceURI(String prefix) {
                    if (prefix == null) return ""
                    if ("xml".equals(prefix)) return "http://www.w3.org/XML/1998/namespace"

                    String uri = doc.lookupNamespaceURI(prefix)
                    if (uri != null && uri.length() > 0) return uri

                    if (prefix.length() == 0) {
                        String defUri = doc.lookupNamespaceURI(null)
                        return defUri != null ? defUri : ""
                    }

                    return rootNsUri != null ? rootNsUri : ""
                }
                String getPrefix(String namespaceURI) { return null }
                Iterator getPrefixes(String namespaceURI) { return null }
            })
        } catch (Exception e) {
            return message
        }
    }

    // ---------------- Apply extraction rules ----------------
    headerDetails.each { hd ->
        String headerName = hd?.HeaderName?.toString()
        def headerPathRaw = hd?.HeaderPath

        if (!headerName || headerPathRaw == null) return

        String headerPath = headerPathRaw.toString().trim()
        if (!headerPath) return

        def values = []

        if (isXml && doc != null && xPath != null) {
            String effectiveXpath = headerPath

            // If XML is namespaced and XPath has no prefix, rewrite to local-name()
            if (rootNsUri && !headerPath.contains(":")) {
                effectiveXpath = toLocalNameXPath(headerPath)
            } else if (!headerPath.startsWith("/") && !headerPath.startsWith("//")) {
                // non-namespaced XML + simple name
                effectiveXpath = "//*[local-name()='" + headerPath + "']"
            }

            try {
                NodeList nodes = (NodeList) xPath.evaluate(effectiveXpath, doc, XPathConstants.NODESET)
                for (int i = 0; i < nodes.length; i++) {
                    def v = nodes.item(i)?.getTextContent()
                    if (v != null) {
                        v = v.toString().trim()
                        if (v) values << v
                    }
                }
            } catch (Exception ignore) {
                // skip
            }
        }

        if (isJson && jsonObj != null) {
            def found = []
            try {
                if (headerPath.startsWith("//") || headerPath.startsWith("\$.")) {
                    found = evalJsonPath(jsonObj, headerPath)
                } else {
                    found = deepFindValuesByKey(jsonObj, headerPath, [])
                }
            } catch (Exception ignore) {
                found = []
            }

            found.each { v ->
                if (v == null) return
                String s = v.toString().trim()
                if (s) values << s
            }
        }

        String finalValue = values.join(",")

        // Set runtime header always (empty string is okay)
        message.setHeader(headerName, finalValue)

        // Write to MPL only if value exists (keeps Monitoring clean)
        if (messageLog != null && finalValue) {
            messageLog.addCustomHeaderProperty(headerName, finalValue)
        }
    }

    return message
}

Step 2: Store JSON config as a Global Variable

Place your JSON global variable.
This becomes the governed source of truth for all monitoring headers.

{
  "root": {
    "Iflow": [
      {
        "IflowID": "OutboundDelivery_-_Sample",
        "HeaderDetails": [
          {
            "HeaderName": "DeliveryNumber",
            "HeaderPath": "DeliveryNumber"
          },
          {
            "HeaderName": "ShippingPoint",
            "HeaderPath": "//ShippingPoint"
          }  
        ]
      },
       {
        "IflowID": "SalesOrder_-_Sample",
        "HeaderDetails": [
          {
            "HeaderName": "SalesOrderNumber",
            "HeaderPath": "SalesOrderNumber"
          }  
        ]
      }
    ]
  }
}

Step 3: Reference JSON in iFlow

Inside the iFlow (OutboundDelivery – Sample):

  • Add a Content Modifier  step
  • Create exchange property:
    • Name: HeaderConfigJson
    • Value: ${globalVariable.GlobalCustomHeaderConfig} (your variable)

Step 4: Add Script Step (Reusable)

Add a Script step and select the script from the Script Collection.

This keeps your iFlow lightweight and centralized.

How it works behind the scenes

1. Identifying the running iFlow

To select the right config, the script reads the runtime iFlow ID using:

SimpleBuilder.simple('${camelId}').evaluate(exchange, String)
 
2. Detecting the payload type (XML vs JSON)

The script inspects the first character:

  • < → XML
  • { or [ → JSON

3. Extracting values

For XML:

  • Parses using DocumentBuilderFactory with namespaceAware = true
  • Supports multiple HeaderPath formats:
    • //DeliveryNumber (deep search)
    • DeliveryNumber (shorthand)
    • Absolute XPath /OutboundDelivery/...
  • Handles namespaces via the local-name() technique when needed
    (XPath is namespace-sensitive; unprefixed paths normally do not match namespaced XML.)

For JSON:

Supports:

  • Deep search (//DeliveryNumber)
  • Shorthand (DeliveryNumber)
  • JSONPath-lite ($.OutboundDelivery.DeliveryHeader.DeliveryNumber)
  • Matching multiple values and joining them

4. Writing to Monitoring

Values are written using:

messageLog.addCustomHeaderProperty(...)
 
What you see in Message Monitoring

After message processing:

Here you’ll see the extracted values (DeliveryNumber, SalesOrderNumber, etc.), which you can use to filter and search messages.

Example with XML (Namespaced)

Payload:

 
<od:OutboundDelivery xmlns:od="urn:example:outbounddelivery:v1">
<od:DeliveryHeader>
<od:DeliveryNumber>80012346</od:DeliveryNumber>
<od:ShippingPoint>0001</od:ShippingPoint>
</od:DeliveryHeader>
</od:OutboundDelivery>
 
Config:
 
{ "HeaderName": "DeliveryNumber", "HeaderPath": "//DeliveryNumber" }
{ "HeaderName": "ShippingPoint", "HeaderPath": "//ShippingPoint" }

Result in monitor:

  • DeliveryNumber = 80012346
  • ShippingPoint = 0001

The script resolves namespaces automatically using prefix-independent matching.

Different Ways to Configure HeaderPath for XML & JSON Inputs:

Payload Type Example Payload Config Example (HeaderPath) How Script Interprets It Expected Monitoring Result
XML (no namespace)<DeliveryNumber>80012345</DeliveryNumber>"//DeliveryNumber"Deep XML search (prefix‑agnostic)DeliveryNumber = 80012345
XML (no namespace)Same as Case 1"DeliveryNumber"Shorthand → auto‑converted to //*[local-name()='DeliveryNumber']DeliveryNumber = 80012345
XML (no namespace)Same"/OutboundDelivery/DeliveryHeader[1]/DeliveryNumber[1]"Absolute XPath; used directlyDeliveryNumber = 80012345
XML (namespaced, prefix = od)**<od:DeliveryNumber>80012346</od:DeliveryNumber>"//DeliveryNumber"Auto‑converted to namespace‑safe XPath using local-name() (because XPath without prefix does not match namespaced XML)DeliveryNumber = 80012346
XML (namespaced, prefix = od)**Same"//od:DeliveryNumber"Script resolves prefix → namespace URI dynamicallyDeliveryNumber = 80012346
XML (namespaced, prefix = ns1)**<ns1:DeliveryNumber> but config uses "//od:DeliveryNumber""//od:DeliveryNumber"Script remaps any prefix to the correct URI based on XML (prefix‑agnostic)DeliveryNumber = value
JSON"DeliveryNumber": "80012345""//DeliveryNumber"Deep JSON search by key name anywhere in the treeDeliveryNumber = 80012345
JSONSame"DeliveryNumber"Shorthand key name → deep searchDeliveryNumber = 80012345
JSONItems array"//ItemNumber"Deep search → collects all occurrencesAllItemNumbers = 000010,000020
JSONSame"$.OutboundDelivery.DeliveryHeader.DeliveryNumber"JSONPath‑lite → exact extractionDeliveryNumber = 80012345
JSON array"DeliveryItems": [{ItemNumber}]"$.OutboundDelivery.DeliveryItems[*].ItemNumber"JSONPath‑lite with wildcard → collect all itemsAllItemNumbers = 000010,000020
JSON + XML mixed iFlowsAnyAny of the aboveScript automatically detects payload type (< vs { or [), then applies appropriate extraction logicAlways extracts correct values

Governance Recommendations:

  • Manage the JSON config as a controlled operational artifact
  • Keep header names business-friendly
  • Put validation rules or whitelists if needed
  • Periodically review which monitoring fields provide actual value

Troubleshooting:

Custom header not showing in MPL?

Ensure you use addCustomHeaderProperty (message headers alone won’t show up).

Value empty for XML?

Namespace mismatch. XPath matching is namespace-sensitive; prefix-independent matching fixes this.

Wrong config?

Double-check IflowID vs ${camelId} mapping (SimpleBuilder evaluation).

Final Thoughts:

This pattern gives both development and operations teams a powerful, flexible, and maintainable way to enhance monitoring without repeatedly modifying or transporting iFlows.

  • Configurable via Global Variables
  • Reusable via Script Collection
  • Monitoring enhanced using MPL Custom Header Properties
  • Supports XML + JSON
  • Safe, governed, and easy to extend

entry.title: Story Report - Filtering Mentors by Availability in Mentoring Programme
entry.content:
Hi all,

I’m hoping someone in the community may have encountered a similar scenario when reporting on Mentoring using Story Reports.

We run an open enrolment mentoring programme, and a number of mentoring relationships are now coming to an end.

Some mentors will be available to take on new mentees, while others have either:

  • indicated they are unavailable for further matches, or
  • have already been matched with a new mentee.

I’m trying to build a Story Report (Table) that identifies mentors who are available to be matched again.

What I can currently do

I can filter the report using:

  • Mentoring End Date to identify relationships that have ended
  • Match Date to identify mentors who currently have no match

This allows me to return mentors who no longer have an active mentoring relationship.

The issue

I also need to exclude mentors who are not available for matching.

Availability appears to be controlled within the Mentor Signup Status / Manager Approved status, where mentor availability or unavailability is stored, but this information doesn’t appear to be directly filterable in Story reports.

I attempted to use Mentor Unavailability End Date as a filter (to include mentors who do not have an end date recorded), however this returns the following error in Story reports.

I attempted to use Mentor Unavailability End Date as a filter (to include mentors who do not have an end date recorded), however this returns the following error in Story Reports.

What I’m trying to achieve

Ideally, the report would return:

Mentors who:

  • previously had a mentoring relationship that has ended
  • do not currently have a new mentee
  • are available for matching

Help

Has anyone found a way to:

  • Filter mentor availability in Story Reports, or
  • Identify mentors available for matching when some may have already been matched again or marked unavailable?

Any thoughts, suggestions much appreciated.

entry.title: Re: Missing API Token management Page In Cloud Portal
entry.content:
Dear Customer,

Following the recent Cloud Portal migration, we want to make you aware of an important breaking change that affects the Cloud Portal API and CLI.

Breaking Change: API & CLI Authentication

As part of the migration, previous authentication methods are no longer supported.

If you are using Cloud Portal API or CLI in any CI/CD pipelines or automation scripts, these will stop working immediately unless updated.

To restore functionality, you must switch to the new Technical User–based authentication.

 

What You Need to Do:

1. Create a Technical User

You can create a Technical User directly in the Cloud Portal:

  1. In the left navigation menu, go to User Management
  2. Select Technical Users
  3. Click Create
  4. Assign Role and Environment Access:
    • Role:
      • CUSTOMER_SYS_ADMIN — full administrative control
      • CUSTOMER_DEVELOPER — development-level access
    • Environment Access: Select the environments available in your subscription

After saving, the system begins creating the technical user.

2. Retrieve the Credentials: Once the technical user is created, a popup will appear containing the required credentials.

Please copy and securely store them immediately, as this is the only time the full set will be displayed.

You will receive:

  • Token Endpoint URL
  • Client ID
  • Client Secret
  • Resource Identifier
  • Expiration Date

3. Authenticate:Use these credentials to request an access token from the provided token endpoint.

This token will replace your old authentication mechanism.

4. Access Cloud Portal APIs:Use the generated token when calling Cloud Portal APIs or when configuring your automation pipelines.

For additional guidance, please refer to:

  • KBA 3707347 – Testing Technical Users in the Cloud Portal
  • Migration of Cloud Portal to Kyma Runtime
  • Technical Users
  • Creating a Technical User

entry.title: What happen in SAP Columbus first Physical meet of 2026?
entry.content:
On February 26, 2026, we had our first meeting of this year. It was a face-to-face meeting. We discussed the AI applications in Supply Chain Maangement and Plant Maintenance. We also discussed about the management of sensors in the Utility industry. We did some planning for SAP INSIDE TRACK which we are planning in month of April. After considering all suggestions, we finalized the date for SIT as 25th April. It will be our first SAP INSIDE TRACK in Columbus. 
We also discussed a pre-event in March which will be a rehearsal for SIT. This event is SAP Innovation Festival. I have posted the event and invited guests there.

entry.title: How should object decomposition be approached in Product Master Data Management within the software
entry.content:
Dear SAP Expert,

As a developer of enterprise management software, I am keen to understand which product master data objects your company manages across systems within the software industry. Additionally, at what level would you recommend defining the smallest manageable unit?

Unlike hardware, software does not have a definitive concept of a "smallest component," as it can be infinitely decomposed—into application suites, features, plug-in packages, lines of code, and so on.

entry.title: SAP CodeJam Roadshow 2026 – Brazil Edition: La Comunidad SAP Paraguay Estuvo Ahí
entry.content:
¡Hola comunidad SAP Paraguay! En marzo de 2026 tuvimos la oportunidad de participar en el SAP CodeJam Roadshow 2026 – Brazil Edition, dos jornadas intensas de aprendizaje hands-on en São Paulo, Brasil. Y como siempre, Paraguay no se quedó afuera.



¿Qué es el SAP CodeJam?
Para quienes aún no lo conocen: el SAP CodeJam es un evento práctico de entre 5 y 6 horas, respaldado por SAP, donde los participantes exploran tecnologías reales en un ambiente colaborativo y sin presión. No se trata de escuchar presentaciones — se trata de hacer, experimentar y aprender juntos.

Esta edición fue parte de un roadshow por Brasil, con paradas en la oficina de SAP en São Paulo y Exed Consulting. Los instructores fueron los Developer Advocates Antonio Maradiaga y Kevin Riedelsheimer — un lujo para cualquier developer SAP.


Los dos dias ¿Qué vivimos?
✔️3 de marzo – Joule Studio: Create Joule Skills and Agents Without Coding
El primer día nos introdujo a Joule Studio, la nueva herramienta dentro de SAP Build que permite extender a Joule, el asistente digital de SAP, con flujos propios.

✔️ 4 de marzo – SAP Build: Create Event-Based Processes
El segundo día fue igualmente intenso. En Exed Consulting, trabajamos con SAP Build Process Automation y SAP Integration Suite, advanced event mesh para construir procesos disparados por eventos.

Lo mas destacado
Más allá del contenido técnico, lo que hace especial a un CodeJam es la gente. Compartir mesa con profesionales de Brasil, resolver ejercicios juntos, debatir enfoques distintos — eso no tiene precio.
Un agradecimiento especial a Kevin Riedelsheimer, Antonio Maradiaga y Antonia Zorn por la confianza y la oportunidad


SAP Community = Conexiones Reales
Eventos como este no llegan solos. Detrás de cada CodeJam hay una comunidad activa que lo hace posible: profesionales que publican en el SAP Community Blog, que comparten sus experiencias, que asisten a eventos en la región, que conectan con Developer Advocates y que demuestran que hay una comunidad con interés real.

No alcanza con querer que suceda: hay que ser parte activa. Eso significa:

  • Crear una cuenta en SAP Community y participar con preguntas, respuestas y blogs
  • Seguir y conectar con los Developer Advocates en LinkedIn y SAP Community
  • Compartir las experiencias de los eventos regionales para que Paraguay aparezca en el radar
  • Asistir a CodeJams en países vecinos y llevar de vuelta el conocimiento.

Desde la SAP Community Paraguay seguimos trabajando para que Ciudad del Este y Asunción sean paradas reales en el mapa de la comunidad SAP de la región.


¿Qué tema te gustaría ver en un CodeJam en Paraguay? ABAP Cloud, Joule Studio, SAP Build, BTP, UI5 — cuéntanos en los comentarios. Cada opinión cuenta para hacerlo realidad.

¡Sigamos construyendo comunidad! 🚀

entry.title: Week 2 : Use Joule Chat to create ABAP RAP Application and explore the Explain feature
entry.content:
Last week we had completed the system set up and also created a package with your community ID. We had also gone through the help document on SAP Joule for Developers, ABAP AI Capabilities.

Now let us dive into the world of ABAP AI by creating an application where Joule will guide you during each step.

Quick Links

Please use this separate thread to ask your questions and discuss issues. 

Task 1: Create a ABAP RAP application using 'OData UI Service from Scratch’ wizard

It can be any application of your choice preferably non enterprise related to keep the challenge simple and more interesting. One field should be an amount field and another one a date field so that we can leverage J4D(Joule for Developers) to add some logic in the subsequent weeks. You should have 3 more fields which will be used to try out some of the J4D features.

Note: It is recommended to create all the fields while creating the app itself to avoid any manual coding later.

Example:- You can create a book store application consisting of fields ID, Title, Author, Price, Published Date, Stock, Discounted Price, BookAge. Here the last 3 fields can be used to try out the Joule for Developers (J4D) features. You are free to choose any dataset and logic combinations.

  1. Right click on the package ZAC_<your community ID> created and select ‘Generate ABAP Repository Objects’Generate ABAP Repository Objects’
  2. In the below popup that appears choose ‘Odata UI Service from Scratch’ and click on ‘Next’ two times.Odata UI Service from Scratch
  3. Click ‘Next’ again.Click Next
  4. In the next screen make sure you get the Joule Help to the right of Wizard popup. Here in the section ‘Ask Joule to update the fields’ can provide your prompts to create the application you like by specifying the fields and other details. Hit enter. Note: It is better to create all objects ending with your <community ID> to avoid any naming conflicts. Try to specify this in the prompt.Joule option in WizardNote: Make sure to select the option -  ‘Transactional with Draft’ highlighted in the above screenshot is selected to enable ‘Create’ option in the application getting created. For single entity sometimes it is set as ‘Without Draft’.
  5. Joule will now propose the Entity and Fields based on your prompt. Please note that we still should verify whether the suggestions are accurate for our requirements. Here you can notice that sometimes Joule suggests to change the Service Configuration to ‘Transactional Without Draft’, we can handle this in next step.Joule Field Proposal
  6. Once you are satisfied click on ‘Accept’ and you can see that the ‘Artifact Suffix’ if specified and the ‘Project Name’ gets added as shown in the below screenshot.           Here remember to change the Service Configuration to ‘Transactional with Draft’ and click ‘Next’.Joule Accept Proposal
  7. Joule lists the artifacts that will be created. Verify them again and click on ‘Next’ and then ‘Finish’ on the next screen. 
  8. Refresh the package and check all the artifacts have been created. Publish the Service Binding and preview the application.
  9. The application will open and should have a ‘Create’ button. Here you can either create a data generator class and add some simple logic to fill data / Use the ‘Create’ option in the app to create some sample records. ( Please create 4-5 entries in any case ). You can also adjust the metadata if you like.Application Created

For more information: https://help.sap.com/docs/abap-cloud/abap-development-tools-user-guide/use-odata-ui-service-from-scratch-wizard?locale=en-US

Task 2: Add a simple logic in the CDS data definition

  1. Add some simple logic in the CDS data definition for example to provide 2% discount on actual Price and fill another field say ‘DiscountedPrice’ with the discounted value for all records in the database.

    Note: The logic and examples are mentioned just for your reference and to make the purpose of the challenge easy to understand. Please feel free to use your own logic and dataset.

  2. Now that one of the fields was enhanced, you may need to recreate the draft table ending with _D### before launching the application again. If you do not face any issue you can skip this step.

    To do that, go to the Project Explorer and open the behavior definition click on the icon as shown on the picture to follow the quick fix. Save and activate the draft table.

    Recreate Draft Table

Task 3: Use Joule->Explain capability to understand code

Use ‘Explain’ capability of Joule to get detailed explanation of the code added.

Hint: Please add few lines of code like a simple CASE statement in CDS data definition to test Explain feature.

You may also watch this video to know more about how to use Joule->Explain feature.

We will be back next week with another feature of Joule AI Capability. Stay Tuned!!

entry.title: はじめてのSAP Analytics Cloud BI - データアナライザ
entry.content:
本ブログシリーズはSAP Analytics CloudのBI機能をつかってダッシュボードやレポートの作成を行う方を対象とし、SAP Analytics CloudのBIにおける基本の操作やTipsをハンズオン形式でご紹介します。

本ブログでは、「ストーリーを見ている時にグラフやテーブルに表示されていない分析軸を含めたより詳細な情報や差異の要因を知りたい」際に分析軸や分析対象の数値項目を追加したり入れ替えたりしながらアドホック分析を行う機能である、データアナライザについてご紹介いたします。

 

目次：

  • データアナライザの概要
  • データアナライザの利用方法[ハンズオン]
    • データアナライザの有効化
    • データアナライザを利用した分析
    • インサイトをストーリーに固定：分析内容をストーリー上にピン留めしてすぐにアクセスできるように設定

※ベースとなるサンプルストーリーの作成⼿順は、はじめてのSAP Analytics Cloud BI - サンプルモデル／ストーリーの作成 - SAP Communityでご紹介しています。
※ SAP Analytics Cloudは、四半期に１度のペースで製品のバージョンアップが実施されます。そのため今後のバージョンアップにより、本ブログで紹介する画⾯キャプチャや、操作が異なる可能性があることをご了承ください。（本ブログは2026年3⽉に作成しています。）

データアナライザの概要

SAP Analytics Cloudのデータアナライザ機能は、分析軸や分析対象の数値項目を追加したり入れ替えたりしながらアドホック分析を行うことで、分析を深めたい部分に関してのチャートやテーブルを追加で作成することなく、ユーザーが即座にアドホックに詳細分析を行うことに役立ちます。

詳しくはHelp Portalもご参照ください：データアナライザ

※データアナライザはストーリーからだけでなく、機能メニューやファイルリポジトリ、JustAsk機能などから利用することも可能です。本ブログではストーリーからの起動についてご紹介します。詳細はHelp Portalをご確認ください： データアナライザの起動

データアナライザの利用方法

0. はじめてのSAP Analytics Cloud BI - サンプルモデル／ストーリーの作成 - SAP Communityで作成したストーリーを編集モードで開こう！

0.1. ストーリーのナビゲーションバーから「編集」をクリックします。

 

1. データアナライザを有効化しよう！

1.1. 対象のチャートまたはテーブルをクリックし、右パネル（ビルダパネル）を開きます。

 

1.2 パネルメニューをスクロールダウンし、「チャートのプロパティ」＞「表示モード」から「データアナライザを有効化」にチェックをつけます。

※チャートやテーブルからのデータアナライザの利用設定はストーリー設定から一括で有効化することも可能です。一括で有効化したい場合には「ストーリー設定」＞「ストーリー詳細」＞「データ探索の設定」を有効化します。

 

1.3. ストーリーを保存し、ストーリーを「表示モード」に切り替えます。

 

2. データアナライザを使って分析しよう！

2.1. 詳細に分析したいチャートまたはテーブルをクリックし、メニューから「データアナライザを開く」を選択します。（「現在のタブで開く」「新規タブで開く」は任意に選択ください）

 

2.2. データアナライザが開かれます。画面には2.1で選択したチャートのデータが表示されています。メニューバーのフィルタボタンを押すと、ストーリーのチャートで利用されていたフィルタが引き継がれていることが確認できます。

 2.3. 画面右の「≪（展開）」ボタンをクリックし、ビルダパネルを開きます。

 

2.4. 追加したい項目を「利用可能なオブジェクト」から行または列のアイコンをクリックするかドラッグ＆ドロップで「ビルダ」に追加し、ディメンションやメジャーを追加します。（今回は例として「製品グループ」を行に追加します）

 2.5. 2.4の操作を繰り返しながら分析を行います。行に配置された項目を列にドラッグ＆ドロップで移動したり、項目の並び順を入れ替えることも可能です。また、「表示タイプ」からチャートでの表示に切り替えることも可能です。

操作についての 詳細はHelp Portalもご参照ください：データアナライザ

 

3. データアナライザの分析内容をストーリー上にピン留めしてすぐにアクセスできるように設定

をしよう！

データアナライザのインサイトをストーリーに固定することでデータアナライザの分析内容をストーリー上にピン留めしてすぐにアクセスできるように設定することが可能です。作成したインサイトに直接アクセスすることができるため分析を効率化することができます。

3.1. メニューバーから「保存」を選択します。保存ダイアログで、インサイトの名前を追加し、「保存してストーリーに固定」を選択します。※ストーリーの「表示」モードからデータアナライザを起動する必要があります。

 

3.2 ストーリーに戻り、チャートまたはテーブルをクリックし、メニューから「データアナライザを開く」を選択します。固定されたインサイトが表示されていることが確認できます。固定されたインサイトを選択し、3.1で保存したインサイトに直接アクセスできます。

 

今回はデータアナライザ 機能のご紹介でした。SAP Analytics Cloudは他にも様々な表現や便利な機能があり、本ブログシリーズでご紹介しています。はじめてのSAP Analytics Cloud BI - シリーズまとめ - SAP Communityからご参照ください。

entry.title: Re: SAP Databricks - Data Classification : A Practical Guide for Modern Data Governance
entry.content: @Yogananda  Thanks for this, Data Classification should not this flow in from Datasphere or we will need to do the same again in SAP Databricks. Please can we have this bi directional as well.
